step_by_step_thinking:
I slightly disagree with all current selections but lean closer to Option B (4 minutes), though my preference would ideally align more closely with Option A if feasible. My primary concern is that even 3 minutes might fall short of adequately saturating the alveoli and pulmonary capillaries compared to what research suggests as effective. Studies often recommend around 60 seconds to achieve initial improvements followed by another minute or two to maintain those gains until induction begins. Given practical concerns about inducing positional changes after extensive pre-oxygenation attempts, extending up to 4 minutes seems prudent yet manageable. This duration still keeps us well within recommended guidelines while minimizing risks related to prolonged immobility.

Therefore, **my final selection remains close to but marginally favors Option B**, recognizing its alignment better with contemporary best practices balancing thoroughness with feasibility. Final
